sourceMovie.title: [REC]
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: [REC] works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Ring
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If [REC] clicked for you, The Ring (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after [REC]?
faq.answer: Try The Ring, Sinister, The Blair Witch Project—each shares DNA with [REC] while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is [REC]?
faq.answer: [REC] (2007) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is [REC] part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "[REC] Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
